metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC4: name the sibling story (ticket ID), state whether this AC blocks closure of THIS story or is deferred, and add a concrete proving command (e.g., `<cli-binary> land --help 2>&1 | grep -c 'land' || echo 0 — expect 0`) that can be run once the retire-code story completes.
findings.finding: AC4 says 'coordinate with the retire-code story' for CLI --help / MCP reference doc regeneration, but names no ticket, does not state whether AC4 blocks this story's completion or is conditional, and provides no in-session proving command the executor can run. An agent cannot verify or complete this criterion without knowing the sibling story's identity and status.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'Any CLI --help / drift-gated command+MCP reference docs regenerate clean after the land command is removed (coordinate with the retire-code story).'
findings.suggested_fix: AC4 (revised): 'Any CLI --help / drift-gated command+MCP reference docs regenerate clean after the land command is removed. **Depends on: [retire-code story ticket ID].** Proving command: `<binary> --help 2>&1 | grep -iE "autosubmit|land command" | wc -l` — expect 0. This AC may be verified in the retire-code story's PR; mark this story complete only after that PR merges.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a presence-proving grep for AC2 — e.g., `grep -rliE 'LLM-Review|Verified\+1|Rebase-If-Necessary' docs/ CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md README.md` — expect each listed file to match at least one key phrase from the new model.
findings.finding: AC2 specifies that named files 'describe the Submit-when-green / Gerrit-rebases model' but provides no concrete proving command to verify PRESENCE of the new model's key facts in each file. Only AC1 checks for the ABSENCE of old terms via grep, leaving the positive claim in AC2 unverified mechanically.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'CLAUDE.md, CLAUDE.local.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, your-first-change.md, README.md landing sections describe the Submit-when-green / Gerrit-rebases model.'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC2: 'Verified by: `grep -lE "LLM-Review|Verified\\+1|Rebase-If-Necessary" CLAUDE.md CLAUDE.local.md CONTRIBUTING.md docs/your-first-change.md README.md` — all six files must appear in output.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC1 to state whether infra/ infrastructure files (infra/scripts/*.sh, infra/nginx/, infra/compose/) that contain autolander references are in scope for this docs-sweep story or are excluded because they are removed by the prerequisite bcf0 story; add an explicit exclusion clause or a prerequisite ordering note.
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 ('a fresh grep returns only (a) historical ADRs with a supersession banner, and (b) ADR 0047 itself') is unachievable if infra/ script files remain in the repo after this story lands. Grep confirmed that infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh, infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh, infra/scripts/observability.sh, infra/scripts/config-check.sh, infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template, and infra/compose/docker-compose.yml all contain 'autosubmit', 'autolander', or 'auto-lander' references that are INFRASTRUCTURE artifacts (not docs/guidance) belonging to the bcf0 decommission story — NOT this docs-sweep story. The plan does not state that AC1 should exclude infra/ infrastructure hits, leaving the acceptance criterion ambiguous and likely failing on execution if bcf0 has not already landed.
findings.location: AC1 / What section ('Targets include... grep is source of truth')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: '(infra/ script/config files referencing autolander are excluded from this grep gate — their removal is covered by the bcf0 decommission story, which is a prerequisite of this story)' and add 'Requires: bcf0 landed' to the plan's Prerequisites section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove CLAUDE.local.md from the named sweep targets list (it is .gitignored and does not exist in the repo), or note that it is a per-developer local override that cannot be repo-swept.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'CLAUDE.local.md (local land guidance)' as a named sweep target, but CLAUDE.local.md is listed in .gitignore and does not exist in the repository. There is no file to sweep. The plan cannot update a gitignored, non-existent file.
findings.location: What section — 'CLAUDE.md (Git workflow / land-through-Gerrit section), CLAUDE.local.md (local land guidance)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'CLAUDE.local.md (local land guidance)' with a note: 'CLAUDE.local.md is .gitignored and developer-local — not sweepable; add a note in CLAUDE.md instructing developers to update their local copy if they have one.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge that `CLAUDE.local.md` is git-ignored and per-developer — either remove it from AC2 or replace with an instruction (e.g. a note in CLAUDE.md or CONTRIBUTING.md) telling developers to delete/update their local copy.
findings.finding: CLAUDE.local.md is listed as a named edit target in the What section and AC2, but it is git-ignored (`.gitignore` line 14) and therefore does not exist as a committed repo file. It is a per-developer local file that cannot be edited as part of a repo change, so the plan's 'coordinate a rewrite of CLAUDE.local.md' is a hallucinated edit target for version-control purposes.
findings.location: ## What — comprehensive grep-driven sweep, bullet 1 ('CLAUDE.local.md (local land guidance)'); AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Remove CLAUDE.local.md from AC2 and add a sentence to CLAUDE.md's Git workflow section noting that any local CLAUDE.local.md should delete or replace its `lander_down` / `land <change> --wait` section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit prerequisite dependency on the retire-code story (d0e5) completing before this doc sweep begins, or split AC#4 to make the sequencing assumption explicit (e.g., 'this story runs after d0e5 lands and the land command is absent from the repo').
findings.finding: AC#4 says 'coordinate with the retire-code story' for CLI --help regeneration but does not establish a hard sequencing dependency on the retire-code story (d0e5) completing first. ADR 0047's migration sequences this doc story (step 4) after code deletion (step 2), but if this story lands before d0e5, docs will assert 'no land command / no Autosubmit label' while the code and label still exist; if it lands after, the story's own sweep references docs/land-contract.md that has already been deleted by d0e5 — an ordering assumption that can silently break the sweep. 'Coordinate' is too loose to prevent either failure mode.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC#4; ## What — 'Targets include… docs/land-contract.md (delete/redirect)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: 'Prerequisite: story d0e5 (delete auto-lander code) must be closed before this sweep begins — the grep will then find no live `land` / `land-status` / `land-contract` artifacts in code, confirming the code story is complete, and the doc sweep removes their references.' Add `blocks: d0e5` → wait for d0e5 to close, then claim this story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC4 by naming the retire-code story ticket (d0e5) as an explicit prerequisite and stating whether AC4 is blocking (must be verified before this story closes) or advisory (tracked in d0e5).
findings.finding: AC4 says 'Any CLI --help / drift-gated command + MCP reference docs regenerate clean after the land command is removed (coordinate with the retire-code story)' but names no specific story ticket and does not state whether this is a blocking dependency of this story or a follow-on. The 'land' command exists in infra/autolander/land.py today. AC4's completion depends on the retire-code story (d0e5) having removed the land command first, but the plan gives no evidence that d0e5 is sequenced as a prerequisite — it is merely mentioned as a coordination point.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC4 as: 'Requires d0e5 (retire-code) to have landed first. Once d0e5 is complete: re-run `rebar mcp-reference` and `rebar cli-reference` generation and confirm no land/land-status/land-contract entries appear in the output.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve 'delete/redirect' to a single chosen outcome for docs/land-contract.md and add a covering AC that verifies it (e.g., 'land-contract.md is deleted and any inbound links are updated' or 'land-contract.md redirects to <target> with a supersession note').
findings.finding: The What section describes two possible outcomes for docs/land-contract.md ('delete/redirect') but no acceptance criterion verifies which outcome was chosen or that it was executed — the file could remain untouched and AC#1 would still pass if it contains no grep search-term hits.
findings.location: What section — 'docs/land-contract.md (delete/redirect)'; Acceptance Criteria — AC#1, AC#2

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit sequencing constraint to AC#4 (or the plan body) stating whether this story must precede or follow the retire-code story, and describe the rollback/coordination mechanism if they land out of order.
findings.finding: AC#4 says 'coordinate with the retire-code story' for CLI --help regeneration but specifies no sequencing constraint — if this doc story merges before the code retirement, docs will assert 'no land command' while the live CLI still exposes it (or vice versa), with no rollback/back-out plan for that intermediate state.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC#4; What section — 'Inline: CLI --help text, script comments'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC#4: 'This story MUST NOT merge until the retire-code story has landed on main (or vice-versa — pick one ordering and enforce it via a blocking story dependency); if this story lands first, a follow-up commit removing the --help stub must be tracked as a hard dependency.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit sequencing note to the plan stating whether 5e7a (docs rewrite) should land before or after 03c6 (Gerrit cutover), or add a prerequisite ordering statement so the docs and live Gerrit config are never contradictory for contributors.
findings.finding: The plan states 'There is no bot, no Autosubmit label, no land command' as the new model to document everywhere, but infra/gerrit/project.config still has `action = fast forward only` (line 253) and a full [label "Autosubmit"] block (lines 179-201). The docs-sweep story (5e7a) is step 4 of 6 in the migration — step 6 (03c6, the Gerrit cutover) is what actually removes the Autosubmit label and changes the submit type. If 5e7a and 03c6 are not sequenced correctly, the rewritten docs will describe a model ('Gerrit rebases-and-submits server-side, no Autosubmit label') that contradicts the live Gerrit configuration, misleading contributors during the gap period.
findings.location: New model section / ADR 0047 migration step 4 vs step 6
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add '5e7a should land in the same quiet window as or immediately after 03c6' to the plan, or (b) note that docs are written for the target state and a temporary 'in migration' banner should be added until 03c6 is complete.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to the sweep targets and AC2; update the `land-contract.md` entry (line 108) to remove or replace the auto-lander description when `land-contract.md` is deleted/redirected.
findings.finding: `docs/README.md` (line 108) contains an explicit hyperlink to `land-contract.md` and describes it as 'the auto-lander's agent contract: the `land` / `land-status` outcome enum, exit-code table…'. This file is not listed in the What targets section or AC2, making it an unenumerated consumer that will retain old-model language after the sweep.
findings.location: ## What — comprehensive grep-driven sweep (targets list); AC2
findings.suggested_fix: In `docs/README.md`, replace the `land-contract.md` bullet with a pointer to the new landing section of CONTRIBUTING.md or a brief inline description of Submit-when-green.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove or correct the `workflow/*.md` glob from the targets list — if `.github/workflows/*.yaml` comment strings were intended, name them explicitly; if no such directory was meant, delete the entry.
findings.finding: The What section includes 'any workflow/*.md' as a search target but no `workflow/` directory exists anywhere in the repo — this is a phantom file-glob that will match nothing and may give false confidence that the sweep is complete.
findings.location: ## What — comprehensive grep-driven sweep, last bullet ('docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md, docs/two-vote-gate-rollback.md, docs/releasing.md, review-bot docs, any workflow/*.md')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit task (and AC) to create ADR 0047 documenting the Submit-when-green / Gerrit-rebases model, OR add a cross-reference to the story that creates it so the sequencing dependency is declared.
findings.finding: ADR 0047 is referenced as the authoritative record of the new model (supersession banners on ADR 0040/0042 point to it, and the new model is described inline) but the plan never explicitly creates ADR 0047. If it is produced by a separate story, that dependency is undeclared; if it is expected here, it is missing from the task list and ACs.
findings.location: ADR history section; Acceptance Criteria item 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a task: 'Create docs/adr/0047-submit-when-green.md capturing the new land model, the rationale for dropping the bot, and the rebase-on-conflict procedure' and add a corresponding AC: 'ADR 0047 exists and is linked from ADR 0040/0042 supersession banners.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit grep/search for the file path `land-contract.md` (and any known aliases) to the sweep, and specify the redirect target so links can be updated rather than just deleted.
findings.finding: The grep search terms are all keyword-based and will not detect path/hyperlink references to docs/land-contract.md after it is deleted or redirected. Markdown files (and possibly code) may contain relative links like `[land contract](docs/land-contract.md)` or `[see here](../land-contract.md)` that survive the keyword grep but become dead links.
findings.location: What — comprehensive grep-driven sweep section; docs/land-contract.md bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the search terms to include the literal path string `land-contract.md` and `land_contract.md`, and add to the AC: 'No Markdown or code file contains a hyperlink whose href resolves to the deleted land-contract.md path.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
